hero.headline: I built Lawcidity, an AI-assisted legal search project using Taiwanese court decisions.
hero.subheadline: Turning unstructured court decisions into a usable search product.
hero.supporting: A case study in search architecture, parser refactoring, embedding evaluation, and AI-assisted development.

why_hard.heading: Why This Problem Is Hard
why_hard.intro: Court decisions are difficult to search reliably because their structure varies across judges, case types, and writing styles.
why_hard.card1_body: Important references may be abbreviated, omitted, or expressed inconsistently, which makes reliable normalization difficult.
why_hard.card2_body: Statutes, case references, and legal issues are hard to parse and connect consistently. Errors here can lead to missed citations and weaker retrieval quality.
why_hard.card3_body: A usable search system must balance retrieval quality, latency, and deployment constraints, not just model performance.

what_built.heading: What I Built
what_built.intro: Lawcidity combines parsing, keyword search, and AI-assisted search into a usable interface for exploring Taiwanese court decisions.
what_built.caption: A simplified view of the system from court decisions to keyword and AI-assisted search.

decisions.heading: Key Technical Decisions
decisions.intro: The project was shaped by technical trade-offs across parsing and search workflow design.
decisions.card1_body: I defined expected outputs and used iterative validation to refine normalization logic for messy legal texts.
decisions.card2_body: I restructured the citation parser into smaller, traceable functions to make rule-based behavior easier to inspect, test, and debug.
decisions.card3_body: I compared OpenSearch and PostgreSQL GIN on the same data pool and used the results to guide search architecture decisions.
decisions.card4_body: I designed evaluation cases and compared embedding models based on retrieval quality, speed, and deployment constraints.

contribution.heading: My Contribution
contribution.body: I led the problem framing, evaluation setup, and key technical decisions. AI tools supported parts of implementation, but I validated outputs and made the final trade-offs.

final_cta.heading: Explore the Project
final_cta.body: Read the full case study, inspect the architecture, or try the working demo.
